PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 6 Sub-batch 6.1a SHIPPED ‚Äî Coverage Report PDF content (Page 1 ‚Äî Patrol Index) for /print-render/[tenantSlug]/coverage/[exportId]. Pipeline produces visible Page 1 output (header + 3 summary cards + type subtotal table + 11-column patrol detail table) instead of blank stub. Page 2 (Area Boundary Summary) + Page 3 (Area Covered) ship in 6.1b/6.1c. Period selector implementation foundation laid: packages/shared/src/lib/coverage-period/ (8 pure functions ‚Äî getMonthlyPeriod, getWeeklyPeriod, getAnnualPeriod, getMonthWeekPeriods, getLastCompletedWeek, patrolStartsWithinPeriod, getSelectedTemplatePeriod dispatcher, buildPeriod generic constructor ‚Äî + iso-week helper + tenant-offset support). Period semantics: half-open [start, end), ISO 8601 week numbering, default UTC+8 offset for v2 launch tenants (Mindoro, Banggai). DST audit remains deferred per spec L626. Server query layer apps/web/src/server/coverage-report/ provides getCoverageReportData(tenantSlug, exportId) loader with cross-tenant + reportType guards + tenant-timezone-aware period resolution + patrol fetch with first-segment leaderName + PatrolTrack endpoint extraction.

NEXT:         Open a FRESH Claude Code session and say "Start Batch 6 Sub-batch 6.1b" (or "Show me 6.1b candidates"). **Phase 8 Batch 6 Item 1 (Coverage Report 3-page funder template) is IN PROGRESS** ‚Äî 1 of 3 sub-batches shipped (Page 1 ‚Äî Patrol Index). Remaining work for the Coverage Report:

              **Sub-batch 6.1b ‚Äî Page 2 (Area Boundary Summary):**
              - Enabled-boundary table with patrol counts (assignment via nearestStartArea(patrol.start_location, enabledAreaBoundaries) + featureMatchesArea name/alias fallback per v2 PRODUCT.md ¬ß771 page 2 spec)
              - Map (report-map) overlaying patrol tracks + app-managed boundaries (filled cyan polygons) + optional ArcGIS reference outlines (cyan dashed). Implementation choice: Leaflet client-side render with Puppeteer wait, OR static SVG composition server-side. Lock the decision in DECISIONS_LOG.md.
              - Bar chart of patrol-to-area-boundary distribution (shadcn Chart / Recharts per Rule 14)
              - "Variance Info" inline dialog explaining estimation methodology
              - packages/shared/src/lib/area-attribution/ ‚Äî new module: nearestStartArea (wraps existing findNearestBoundary returning boundary object not just id) + featureMatchesArea (wraps matchByName for {properties.name} ‚Üí AreaBoundary check)
              - Estimated scope: ~14 files, ~50K main-session tokens. Tier 2 MODERATE.

              **Sub-batch 6.1c ‚Äî Page 3 (Area Covered):**
              - Coverage table sorted by coverage_km DESC, columns Boundary Name / Coverage Patrols / Coverage KMS / Coverage HRS, with "Est." badge for rows where hrs_estimated_count > 0
              - Footer note for "Est." rows + missing_tracks count when > 0
              - Bar chart (area-covered-chart) ‚Äî top boundaries by km
              - Empty state: "No coverage in monitored boundaries for this period."
              - **Coverage computation algorithm** (the analytical payoff): for each PatrolTrack LineString, clip against each enabled AreaBoundary polygon ‚Üí accumulate coverage_km per boundary + coverage_hrs (per-point timestamps when hasTimestamps=true, else pro-rated from totalHours with hrs_estimated_count++)
              - Decision lock needed: turf.js dependency vs pure-math line-polygon clipping. Recommend turf.js (well-tested, MIT) ‚Äî add as packages/shared dependency or apps/web dependency
              - Estimated scope: ~12 files, ~55K main-session tokens. Tier 2 MODERATE-HIGH.
